emptyState: Paste or type text above to extract emails, URLs, and phone numbers

descriptions.aeoDefinition: Email & URL Extractor is a free online tool that extracts emails, URLs, and phone numbers from any text instantly. Supports multiple formats and batch processing. Runs entirely in your browser.
descriptions.whatIs: The Text Extractor scans any text and pulls out email addresses, URLs, and phone numbers in a single pass. Toggle each type on or off, view deduplicated results, and export as CSV or JSON — all in your browser.
descriptions.stepsTitle: How to Extract Emails, URLs, and Phone Numbers
descriptions.step1Text: Paste or drop a text file containing emails, URLs, or phone numbers. The tool scans in real time.
descriptions.step2Text: Use the Email, URL, and Phone chip buttons to include or exclude each type. All matches are found in a single pass.
descriptions.step3Text: Copy all results or export as CSV and JSON. Results are deduplicated by default.
descriptions.useCasesP1: Extract email addresses from web pages, documents, or log files for outreach campaigns.
descriptions.useCasesP2: Pull URLs or phone numbers from unstructured text and export them in a structured format.
descriptions.formatsUrlDesc: http:// and https:// — paths, query strings, fragments
descriptions.formatsPhoneDesc: International formats — country code, parentheses, dashes, dots, spaces. Examples: +1 (800) 555-1234, 090-1234-5678, +44 20 7946 0958, 202.555.0123, (02) 2345-6789
descriptions.tip1: Extraction is permissive, not validation — all plausible matches are returned
descriptions.tip2: Toggle individual types on/off with the chip buttons
descriptions.tip3: Results are deduplicated by default; enable "Show duplicates" to see all occurrences
descriptions.tip4: Phone numbers must contain at least 7 digits to be recognized
descriptions.faq1Q: What does the Text Extractor do?
descriptions.faq1A: Extracts specific patterns from text: emails, URLs, and phone numbers. Results are deduplicated by default and can be exported as TXT, CSV, or JSON.
descriptions.faq2Q: Can I define custom extraction patterns?
descriptions.faq2A: Toggle the Email, URL, and Phone chip buttons to include or exclude each type. All matches are found in a single pass.
descriptions.faq3A: Email addresses (supports +tags and subdomains), URLs (http/https with paths, queries, and fragments), and international phone numbers (at least 7 digits). Each type can be toggled independently.
